ddls:
CREATE TABLE "reservoirs" (
  "reservoir_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reservoir */,
  "reservoir_name" TEXT /* Name of the reservoir */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the reservoir */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Total capacity of the reservoir in cubic meters */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the reservoir in meters */,
  "surface_area" REAL /* Surface area of the reservoir in square meters */,
  "catchment_area" REAL /* Area that drains into the reservoir in square kilometers */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last inspection */,
  "next_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled inspection */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the reservoir is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reservoir_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drawdown_periods" (
  "drawdown_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each drawdown period */,
  "reservoir_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reservoir experiencing the drawdown */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the drawdown period */,
  "valley_date" TEXT /* Date when the reservoir level was at its lowest */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the drawdown period */,
  "drawdown_percentage" REAL /* Percentage decrease in water level during the drawdown */,
  "duration_days" INTEGER /* Number of days the drawdown period lasted */,
  "cause" TEXT /* Cause of the drawdown */,
  "recovery_rate" REAL /* Rate at which the reservoir is recovering after the drawdown */,
  "predicted_end_date" TEXT /* Predicted end date of the drawdown */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the drawdown period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("drawdown_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_drawdown_periods_reservoir_id FOREIGN KEY ("reservoir_id") REFERENCES reservoirs ("reservoir_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "water_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each water level record */,
  "reservoir_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reservoir */,
  "record_date" TEXT /* Date the water level was recorded */,
  "water_level" REAL /* Water level in meters */,
  "inflow_rate" REAL /* Rate of water inflow at the time of the record */,
  "outflow_rate" REAL /* Rate of water outflow at the time of the record */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of the record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_water_levels_reservoir_id FOREIGN KEY ("reservoir_id") REFERENCES reservoirs ("reservoir_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inflow_outflow" (
  "flow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inflow/outflow record */,
  "reservoir_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reservoir */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the inflow/outflow */,
  "inflow" REAL /* Volume of water entering the reservoir in cubic meters */,
  "outflow" REAL /* Volume of water leaving the reservoir in cubic meters */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the inflow */,
  "destination" TEXT /* Destination of the outflow */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of the record */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the inflow/outflow */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flow_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inflow_outflow_reservoir_id FOREIGN KEY ("reservoir_id") REFERENCES reservoirs ("reservoir_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, manager, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the user has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "actions_taken" TEXT /* Actions taken by the user during the session */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to access the system */,
  "duration_minutes" INTEGER /* Duration of the user's session in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
